programme.desc: Megan's mother, Shelby (guest star SHARON LAWRENCE - "NYPD Blue"), who abandoned the family when Megan was a child, returns. Will is jealous when Megan turns to Charlie for advice.

programme.title: Privileged
programme.desc: Megan confronts her mother, Shelby (SHARON LAWRENCE), Rose and Sage throw an elaborate charity dinner and Marco's boyfriend, Keith (DAVID MONAHAN), pushes Marco to quit his job.

programme.title: Privileged
programme.desc: Megan wants Lily out of jail, but her father (JOHN ALLEN NELSON) thinks Lily should be left there to learn a lesson. Shelby (SHARON LAWRENCE) later asks Will for the bail money.

programme.title: Privileged
programme.desc: Rose's boyfriend, Zach (DAVID FRANCO), gets upset after seeing a picture of Rose and Sage with bassist PETE WENTZ of the band Fallout Boy. Megan meets Will's parents.

programme.title: Privileged
programme.desc: Charlie surprises Megan by admitting he has been in love with her for years. Will reacts badly to the news. And Rose gets some surprising news of her own.

programme.title: Privileged
programme.desc: The twins confront Laurel with their shocking news. Rose knows Sage is keeping something from her and when Sage finally confesses her secret, it changes their relationship.

programme.title: Privileged
programme.desc: KATHY GRIFFIN appears as Keith (DAVID MONOHAN) and Marco's wedding planner. Megan and Will fight and break up, leaving Megan solo at the wedding. But does she leave solo?

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: In the series premiere, when a series of vampire-style murders plagues Los Angeles, Mick sets out to find the culprits, and reconnects with Beth.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: When a convicted murderer who knows Mick's secret is released from prison, Mick fears Beth will find out.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Still reeling from Mick's revelation about his identity, a cautious Beth struggles as she helps him track down a newly turned vampire who doesn't know how to control his bloodlust.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick gets injured while searching for a woman in the desert. As the sunlight depletes Mick's vampiric ability to heal himself, Beth must rush to take measures to save his life.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick hunts a teenage vampire who is preying on female escorts he locates via the Internet. Meanwhile, Mick and Beth try to avoid each other after sharing an intimate moment.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick searches for a mysterious vampire from Josef's past. Meanwhile, Beth investigates when a model overdoses and dies. When the two cases intersect, Mick and Beth work together.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick experiences déjà vu while investigating a fire that is eerily similar to the one that killed his ex-wife, Coraline.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick protects a frightened woman from the cult followers of a killer after they vow to avenge their leader's dying wish that those responsible for his execution be put to death.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Beth feels slighted and jealous when Mick begins working closely on a case with a mysterious photographer who resembles his ex-wife.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: When a dying man with a long-standing vendetta puts a hit out on Josef, Mick discovers the real reason Josef believes Mick and Beth can never work.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick and Beth must work together when a vindictive drug lord kidnaps Josh and his fate lies in their hands.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick's dream of becoming human again is within reach when he uncovers Coraline's secret. Still angry with Mick, Beth must deal with some heartbreaking news alone.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: When Beth is kidnapped, Mick makes a big decision.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: A Hollywood star hires Mick after being scared by paparazzi.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: Mick faces old secrets; Beth learns more about his past.

programme.title: Moonlight
programme.desc: When a vampire kills a mortal, Mick and Beth face reality.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: In the series premiere of this Pretty Little Liars spin-off, Caleb (TYLER BLACKBURN) opts to stay in mysterious Ravenswood to help out a new pal, Miranda.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: Caleb comes face to face to with the last person he ever expected to see. Remy begins to draw troubling parallels between recent events and painful chapters in Ravenswood history.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: The gang decides to hold a séance to prove to Luke that something otherworldly is happening. Miranda begins to feel like someone or something is watching her.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: Caleb meets a long-lost relative, but is caught between new and old loyalties. Olivia bonds with Miranda and is surprised to discover their families may have a shared history.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: The gang makes discoveries both horrifying and intriguing while investigating what they're up against. Pretty Little Liars' ASHLEY BENSON appears as Caleb's girlfriend.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: Caleb returns from Rosewood. Remy realizes her dreams may be the best clue they have to understand the pact. Miranda decides to haunt Mrs. Grunwald (MEG FOSTER) for answers.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: An ominous discovery is made at the Matheson house. Caleb's dad (BERNARD CURRY) visits. Miranda gains insight into Grunwald (MEG FOSTER) and her connection to the supernatural.

programme.title: Ravenswood
programme.desc: Remy's dreams become more terrifying than ever and Miranda may be the only one who can help her. Caleb turns to Mr. Price (JUSTIN BRUENING) for a lesson in Ravenswood town history.

programme.title: Bloomberg The Opening Trade
programme.desc: The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Kriti Gupta.

programme.title: The Pulse with Francine Lacqua

programme.title: Bloomberg Brief

programme.title: Bloomberg Surveillance
programme.desc: Jonathan, Lisa and Annmarie survey the economy and global markets with daily conversations with leaders and decision makers, from Wall Street to Washington and beyond, to cover the latest in business, investment and geopolitics.

programme.title: Bloomberg Open Interest
programme.desc: Delivering the market-moving news anyone needs to know to get a jump start on the U.S. trading day.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology" takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the U.S. markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled "Bloomberg West," is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Financier and philanthropist David Rubenstein talks with his peers -- leaders of some of America's biggest companies -- in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader. Rubenstein uses his chats to explore successful leadership skills through the personal and professional choices made by the business world's most influential people. Rubenstein travels across the country to find out about the business leaders' stories and uncover information about their paths to success. The interview subjects include Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Berkshire Hathaway chairman Warren Buffett, and Pepsi CEO Indra Nooyi.

programme.title: Leaders with Lacqua
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua interviews some of the most influential people on the planet.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Asia
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets" banner, with each focusing on that geographic area's markets. This Asian-focused version is hosted by Shery Ahn, David Ingles, Haidi Lun, Rishaad Salamat and Angie Lau. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make investment decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Las viejas del sicario
programme.desc: Una película de acción de narcos.

programme.title: Mariachi gringo
programme.desc: Edward, un apasionado por la música mexicana, se siente atrapado en la rutina de la pequeña ciudad donde reside. Con el propósito de huir de todo, Edward se muda a México para convertirse en un mariachi.

programme.title: Soy de la frontera verde
programme.desc: Don Hugo un poderoso narcotraficante, muere en circunstacias muy extrañas, aunque todo indica que lo envenenaron. Su hija, doña Fernanda Morales, no dejará cabos sueltos y vengará la muerte de su padre encontrando al culpable.

programme.title: Un ángel en peligro
programme.desc: La felicidad de una familia se quiebra cuando secuestran a su hija. Al mismo tiempo, se descubrirá la red de engaños entre su sobrino y su esposa, además de un nexo con un narcotraficante.

programme.title: A ellas les gustan mayores
programme.desc: Ricardo está casado con Miranda, una bella mujer de afortunada posición social. Sin embargo, Ricardo la engaña con una mujer más joven que él.

programme.title: Cigarrito Bañado
programme.desc: Lucio huye de una deuda y de un amigo peligroso. En el camino conoce a Ximena, una mujer que seduce a los hombres y luego les roba el dinero.

programme.title: Cometa
programme.desc: Un chico pierde todas las cosas que lo motivan a vivir. Se ha quedado sin trabajo, casa y familia, pero en su recorrido por las calles se encuentra a Cometa, un perro callejero.

programme.title: A ellas les gustan mayores
programme.desc: Ricardo está casado con Miranda, una bella mujer de afortunada posición social. Sin embargo, Ricardo la engaña con una mujer más joven que él.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Yesim ve a Cüneyt en una cafetería y hablan sobre el artículo de Melissa. Metin y Mahinur ven a Serkan, el marido de Sermin, en su jardín buscando pruebas. Kadir busca a Yesim en el club, pero se pone celoso al verla con Cüneyt.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Alicia Vega, psicóloga del colegio Los Ángeles, especialista en prevención y tratamiento de abusos sexuales a menores, inicia una investigación cuando una alumna parece estar en la mira de un abusador.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: En un descuido de Said, Jade saca el pasaporte y dinero para irse de Marruecos. Albieri se siente acorralado, habla con Dora y le promete cumplir sus sueños si ella acepta irse a Los Ángeles.

programme.title: Apuesta Final
programme.desc: Dae-gil, un príncipe obligado a vivir como un plebeyo, arriesga su vida cuando usa sus habilidades de juego para participar en una partida contra el rey Yeongjo, quien apuesta todo el Reino de Joseon.

programme.title: Sol naciente
programme.desc: Mario y Alicia son amigos de la infancia, él es nieto de migrantes italianos y ella es adoptada por Tanaka, un japonés. Cuando Alicia decide estudiar dos años en Japón, Mario se da cuenta de que lo que siente por ella es más que una amistad. Luego del reencuentro él tiene que lidiar con otro obstáculo, César, que trata de enamorar a la joven, pero con oscuras intenciones. Entonces, los Di Angelo y los Tanaka ponen a prueba sus sentimientos a pesar de las traiciones, las estafas y las mentiras.

programme.title: Orgullo y pasión
programme.desc: Elisabeta es una joven rebelde y llena de sueños que lucha contra el único destino para una mujer de buena familia en su época: casarse. Su osadía y su independencia la meterán en más de un problema.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: La historia del pueblo hebreo tras la muerte de Moisés cuando, liderados por Josué, tendrán que enfrentarse a distintos reinos para tomar Canaán, la tierra que Dios les prometió.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: La historia del pueblo hebreo tras la muerte de Moisés cuando, liderados por Josué, tendrán que enfrentarse a distintos reinos para tomar Canaán, la tierra que Dios les prometió.

programme.desc: Elisabeta es una joven rebelde y llena de sueños que lucha contra el único destino para una mujer de buena familia en su época: casarse. Su osadía y su independencia la meterán en más de un problema.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: La historia del pueblo hebreo tras la muerte de Moisés cuando, liderados por Josué, tendrán que enfrentarse a distintos reinos para tomar Canaán, la tierra que Dios les prometió.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: La historia del pueblo hebreo tras la muerte de Moisés cuando, liderados por Josué, tendrán que enfrentarse a distintos reinos para tomar Canaán, la tierra que Dios les prometió.

programme.title: Apuesta Final
programme.desc: Dae-gil, un príncipe obligado a vivir como un plebeyo, arriesga su vida cuando usa sus habilidades de juego para participar en una partida contra el rey Yeongjo, quien apuesta todo el Reino de Joseon.

programme.title: Sol naciente
programme.desc: Mario y Alicia son amigos de la infancia, él es nieto de migrantes italianos y ella es adoptada por Tanaka, un japonés. Cuando Alicia decide estudiar dos años en Japón, Mario se da cuenta de que lo que siente por ella es más que una amistad. Luego del reencuentro él tiene que lidiar con otro obstáculo, César, que trata de enamorar a la joven, pero con oscuras intenciones. Entonces, los Di Angelo y los Tanaka ponen a prueba sus sentimientos a pesar de las traiciones, las estafas y las mentiras.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-yi descubre lo que probablemente sucederá si Min-joon se queda en la Tierra. Hwi-kyung y Min-joon intentan obligar a Jae-kyung a entregarse.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Melissa quiere una fiesta de té en casa. Serkan le cuenta a Hazim que una niña vio a Besime hablando por teléfono con Sermin. Melissa y Defne beben alcohol.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Marisa se sale con la suya y obtiene un supuesto regalo de Leonardo: la joya de Jade; luego se aprovecha para dar de qué hablar a la prensa. Jade cambia de identidad, roba un bolso.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Rafael y Sofía involucran a Francisco en la misteriosa muerte de Diana. Mateo sabe quién es el Gato, pero no dice nada.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Said le confiesa a Jade que ha tratado de sacarla de su corazón, sin lograrlo. Ella se conmueve, baila la danza del sable y viven una noche de pasión. Lucas se molesta por el collar de Marisa.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: Unsolved murders, romance scams, mysterious disappearances. Each week we'll feature high-profile crime stories from NY, NJ and CT as told by the victims, detectives and our journalists.

programme.title: Crime Files

programme.title: Crime Files

programme.title: Crime Files

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: For years, a powerful New Jersey sheriff sexually abused kids in custody. County officials were warned but did nothing to stop it. News12 unravels three decades of secrets and follows the victims on their quest for justice.

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: In 2016, Sara Stern's car was found on a bridge in Belmar, NJ, leading to speculation she had committed suicide. It was later revealed her childhood friend, Sean McAtasney, had murdered her for her inheritance money. He later confessed to the crime..

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: In a drugstore robbery gone wrong, prescription drug addict David Laffer commits one of the worst mass shootings in Long Island's history as he kills a pharmacist, a 71-year-old clerk, and two shoppers.

programme.title: Crime Files

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: A former Westchester high school football star convicted of killing a rival nearly two decades ago speaks out for the first time. He sits down with Senior Investigative Reporter Tara Rosenblum at Woodbourne Correctional Facility for an exclusive.

programme.title: Crime Files

programme.title: Crime Files

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In Massachusetts it is illegal to frighten a pigeon and it was once forbidden to use tomatoes in clam chowder (sorry, New York)! Massachusetts is arguably the most progressive state in the nation, but for such a seemingly progressive place, the Commonwealth has some weird old laws on the books. Anthony Everett breaks down a list of the state’s unusual blue laws tonight.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein loves diners and he will travel pretty much anywhere in New England to find a good one. Tonight, a look at some of Ted’s (and Chronicle’s) favorite stops for corned beef hash, pancakes – even, yes, a grilled donut!

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5

programme.title: Juvenile Lifers: Road to Redemption
programme.desc: (2019) Juvenile Lifers: Road to Redemption focuses on people who were sentenced to life in prison as juveniles. WGAL News 8's Matt Barcaro examines how these these former offenders are re-entering society and the reaction from victims' families.

programme.title: The Long Road Home
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, the communities of Western North Carolina were forever changed. "The Long Road Home" is a WXII 12 original documentary that captures powerful stories of loss, hope, and recovery.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Local authors, big-time books! Tonight we talk to Boston native and New York Times bestselling author William Martin about his latest historical novel, a World War II thriller called “December ’41”. Natasha Lance Rogoff shares the amazing (true) story of making Sesame Street in Russia, described in her book, “Muppets in Moscow.” We hit Cape Cod for a chat with novelist Keith Yocum, and a visit to the beloved Yellow Umbrella Bookstore. We also have the story behind Storytime Crafts, which promotes equal access to books with book donations and story-themed reading chairs for kids.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Winter is no excuse for hiding indoors – not when there are outdoor adventures to experience. Tonight Nicole Estaphan takes a hike with a duo (human and rabbit) who climb New England peaks together, then she ventures into the New Hampshire night air on a dogsled. At Crane Beach in Ipswich we meet kayakers and horseback riders for whom winter is no obstacle - and keep our eyes peeled for snowy owls. We also take in a performance of spoken word poetry at an expansive outdoor space.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Boston Globe sports columnist Dan Shaughnessy remembers the glory days of the Boston Celtics with his new book, “Wish It Lasted Forever: Life with the Larry Bird Celtics.” If you’re a music lover, Bill Lichtenstein recalls the wild, early days at Boston rock radio station WBCN with “WBCN and the American Revolution.” Also tonight, are you in the mood to cook? We have fish tales – and a cooking demo! – from Row 34 chef and author of a new cookbook, Jeremy Sewall.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, onetime revolutionary Katherine Anne Power talks about her memoir, “Surrender.” Also tonight, we sit down with risk analyst (and NewsCenter 5 alum) David Ropeik about his new book, “Cancerphobia,”

programme.title: WCVB On The Record
programme.desc: Ruthzee Louijeune joins On The Record to discuss politics.

programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: WCVB

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: For 70 years the handsome art deco tower of the old Hancock building in Boston has doubled as a weather beacon. And how many major cities can boast a 40-foot tall milk bottle in the heart of downtown? Tonight Chronicle highlights some of the Hub’s most treasured icons, including the Chinatown gate, the Paul Revere statue, the Zakim bridge, and the Make Way for Ducklings statues in the Public Garden.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB City Line
programme.desc: Building generational wealth and community on Martha's Vineyard. But first, activism and political engagement have always been a part of the inner workings of black sororities and fraternities.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Got the winter blues? Tonight replace them with the clear blue waters of the Bahamas. Shayna Seymour is island-bound, with a visit to thriving Nassau as the Bahamas celebrates its 50th year of independence. Shayna suits up for a lesson in cricket (an island obsession), digs in at a Bahamian fish fry, communes with iconic pink flamingos, braves a state-of-the-art water park, and learns about the tradition of “Junkanoo.” If a winter getaway wasn’t in your plans this year, Shayna’s bringing the tropical breezes and rum punch to you.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: We take in the crisp Fall air with a visit to Southern Vermont’s “flower child” - the funky town of Brattleboro. Speaking of flowers, one local business owner loves Brattleboro so much he volunteers his time to plant more than 200 baskets of flowers downtown every year. Tonight, Shayna Seymour meets local farmers, takes in the beauty of Wantastegok River, and meets a Somerville native bringing treasure-hunting to the Green Mountain State. Finally, what would a trip to Vermont be without a cold craft beer?

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.desc: Covered bridges may be quintessentially New England, but you don't need to venture up to VT or NH to find these beauties. There are some classic covered bridges right here in the Commonwealth. Join Anthony Everett as he sets out on the Great Massachusetts Covered Bridge Road Trip, with some fun [and tasty] stops along the way.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Celebrating a century of hard hits, dramatic moments, and Boston’s love for the Black & Gold. The Bruins have begun their 100th season, and from team ownership to current and former players and fans, we are hitting the ice. Ted Reinstein sits down with the team’s longest-serving employee, who just happens to be Bs legend Johnny Bucyk. Nicole Estaphan chats with father-son duo, John and Matt Grzelcyk, who are making an impact before and during games. Anthony Everett hears what turning 100 means to Weymouth native Charlie Coyle and his family. And we meet a World War II vet who has been rooting for the team for, well, his entire

programme.desc: Ted Reinstein makes a spring visit to Maine’s rocky coast and explores the historic town bounded by Casco Bay and the Androscoggin River.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Where’s the best place to score a burger? According to our informal poll of Chronicle viewers, it’s your own kitchen! Since we can’t visit you all at home we set out to find in-demand burgers around the state. A burger “influencer” guides us to two of his top picks in Boston, and we stop by another spot proclaimed best in the city. We hit a couple of “viewers’ choice” options west of Boston, devour a patty melt at a classic diner, and not to worry – we didn’t forget the non-meat-eaters. There’s a fish burger, and a veggie burger even a meat eater would love.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, Ted Reinstein is off to the “other Cape” – Cape Ann. Thirty miles north of Boston, it is relatively quiet, a mix of seacoast and rural terrain and beautiful, historic towns. Ted checks in on the fishing fleets in Gloucester, visits “Dog Town,” and hangs out with locals at Hula Moon in Rockport. He stops by a historic library in Essex, drives down Cape Ann’s “Clam Highway,” and stops for lunch at the famous Clam Box in Ipswich.

programme.desc: Dogs get plenty of air time on Chronicle but the cat lobby is relentless – so tonight we cave in to offer equal feline time.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Black History Month is an opportunity to highlight the thinkers, artists, and everyday people who helped shape history – but whose stories may not be familiar to all. We meet the grandson of a world-renowned sculptor who made her home right nearby in Framingham, and talk to the Harvard professor whose new book profiles the first Black female federal judge in the United States. Shayna Seymour talks to Ted Reinstein about his new book that highlights the unsung local heroes who helped break Major League Baseball’s color barrier. And we visit Lynn and Cambridge, which are digging more deeply into the history of their Black communities.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Shift: The Ragbrai Documentary
programme.desc: A celebration of the oldest, largest, and longest recreational touring bicycle ride in the world. Three riders and a pair of community leaders reach new personal heights, changing their lives along the seven day ride across Iowa.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Politics, culture…and community. In honor of National Hispanic Heritage Month, NewsCenter 5’s Nathalie Pozo and Jessica Brown help celebrate the individuals and organizations who help make Massachusetts great. In Chelsea, we meet trailblazing Latinas helping to shape their city’s future as elected and community leaders. We sample traditional Peruvian cuisine at Tambo 22, and reimagined empanadas at Buenas and Super Bien. Also tonight we meet local artists who are making their mark, and a family who came to Massachusetts seeking care for their sick baby -- and found both a community and a surprise calling: beekeeping!

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We hit the practice field with the New England Revolution captain Carles Gil and Cranston, R.I., native Damian Rivera. On the food scene, we find Colombian specialties in Lowell and Mexican in Cambridge. And we profile a local party planner, a giant on the fashion scene, as well as longtime journalist and community activist, Jose Massó, a presence on the Boston radio airwaves for nearly 50 years. Tonight, NewsCenter 5’s Jessica Brown, Jennifer Peñate, and Angel Salcedo team up to bring us local stories of achievement in cel

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As the summer heat sizzles, we’re seeking relief! Tonight it’s a tour of Boston Harbor -- today a draw for boaters, a backdrop for outdoor dining, and a destination for tourists and residents alike. But it took decades, billions, and enormous political will to clean up the harbor and spark its economic potential. We check out the progress (a dog bakery on the Fish Pier!) and problems (flood risk); visit Spectacle Island; and meet a third-generation fisherman still making it on the working waterfront.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Youth sports is a multi-billion dollar industry. Tonight Shayna Seymour talks to players, families, and coaches about the pros – and cons – of the modern approach to youth sports, including private club sports.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett is back with more stories from the shore – lake, ocean, and otherwise.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: WCVB

programme.title: Chronicle: Cheap Eats
programme.desc: It’s always a treat to enjoy a meal prepared by someone else, but money’s tight. So we’re shopping for affordable (and tasty) dining-out options! We find entrees in Chinatown for $13, Lebanese/Armenian specialties in Boston for $11, and noodles in the ’burbs for 10 bucks. After 30 years in business, downtown Boston favorite Chacarero is still slinging sandwiches that can stretch to two meals. We hit Casey’s in Natick, a classic diner offering a $3.75 hot dog. And tune in to find out where you can find a burger, fries, and a beer for just 12 bucks.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

For those of you who missed those years, and those who miss the Chevy (and Peter), tonight is a look back.

programme.desc: Join WGAL News 8's Anne Shannon for "Behind the Lens: A Storytellers Project," where we relive the best of 2024 through the eyes of our award-winning photojournalists.

programme.title: Only In
programme.desc: Every city has its own local flavor, culture and vibe. Only In celebrates communities across America and the people, activities and history that make each place unique. From must-try activities to hidden gems and hotspots, to dedicated changemakers, these stories are sure to inspire a deeper love and appreciation for your hometown and beyond.

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Join New Hampshire-based band Recycled Percussion as they perform acts of kindness in their community, and across the country. In each episode, you'll witness the great lengths and hilarious things they're willing to do in the name of kindness.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: Makers, crafters and artists square off to create the most iconic art piece that best represents their city. From glass-blowers and chalk artists to metalsmiths and tattooers, see who Host Ashley Adams and the judges will crown the victor.

programme.title: LIVE: WGAL News 8
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WGAL News 8 team.

programme.title: The Middle Child
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a serial killer buried a woman and three girls in the woods of New Hampshire. One of those girls is still unidentified. WMUR joins the investigators who promise they won’t give up until her name is restored.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: In 1990, an 18-year-old driving across Wisconsin to a job conference never arrives. After her van and later her body are discovered, the mystery surrounding Berit Beck’s death persists for more than two decades. What happened in that van?
